engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz Sprinter across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The W906 workhorse replacing older 2.7L units. Common-rail efficiency defined modern commercial van logistics.
compatibleVariants: 208 CDI, 211 CDI, 308 CDI, 311 CDI, 313 CDI, 411 CDI, 413 CDI, 511 CDI, 513 CDI
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner seal leak (REC-005099 recall, typically 60k-90k miles)
Injector seal blow-by leading to severe cylinder head carbon buildup
EGR cooler cracking under sustained high thermal load
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651.955 Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: Facelift evolution with updated BlueTEC hardware. Higher boost mapping optimised for heavy commercial payloads.
compatibleVariants: 214 CDI, 314 CDI, 316 CDI, 414 CDI, 416 CDI, 514 CDI, 516 CDI, BlueTEC trims
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing cold start hesitation
Timing chain guide rail degradation and noise
Turbocharger wastegate actuator sticking at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651.958 Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Downsized 2.0L aluminium block replacing the 2.1L. Euro 6 compliant with twin-turbo options for newer vans.
compatibleVariants: 214 CDI, 314 CDI, 316 CDI, 414 CDI, 416 CDI, 516 CDI, VS30 generation models
commonFailures: Low-pressure EGR valve and cooler clogging (frequent fault codes)
Camshaft phaser rattle on cold starts
DPF differential pressure sensor failure causing restricted regeneration
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654.920 Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel V6
entries.history: Premium 3.0L V6 adapted for heavy-duty Sprinters. Smooth power delivery suited high-spec passenger conversions.
compatibleVariants: 319 CDI, 419 CDI, 519 CDI, Marco Polo camper conversions
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal failure causing blue exhaust smoke
Injector cup leaks leading to coolant contamination
Oil cooler gasket deterioration around 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642.896 Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel V6
entries.history: Updated V6 calibration meeting Euro 5 standards. Maintains high torque output for heavy towing applications.
compatibleVariants: 319 CDI BlueTEC, 519 CDI BlueTEC, high-spec passenger shuttles
commonFailures: EGR swirl flap mechanism jamming and fault logging
Turbo bearing wear from extended urban idling cycles
Intake manifold carbon restriction affecting throttle response
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642.898 Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol/CNG
entries.history: Dedicated Natural Gas Technology unit. Dual-fuel flexibility reduced operating costs for urban delivery fleets.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch causing catastrophic valve damage (pre-2013 units)
CNG injector seal degradation and fuel trim imbalance
Crankcase ventilation valve blockage increasing oil consumption
entries.cta: Get quotes for M271.951 Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement

entries.title: Electric Drive Unit
entries.history: High-voltage traction motor for eSprinter. Zero-emission urban logistics capability with instant torque.
compatibleVariants: eSprinter, eSprinter Panel Van, eSprinter Tourer
commonFailures: High-voltage battery module degradation reducing range
Inverter coolant pump failure triggering thermal shutdown
Reduction gear bearing wear in high-mileage city operations

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Timing Chain Failure - Why OM651 Engines Rattle and Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 211 CDI, 313 CDI, 316 CDI, 516 CDI (2006-2018, OM651.950/OM651.955/OM651.958)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain tensioner seal degrades under thermal cycling, losing oil pressure and allowing chain slack. This leads to catastrophic guide rail breakage and potential valve train damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete timing kit swap with updated tensioner seal and reinforced guide rails before catastrophic damage occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves rattle completely; viable only if caught before chain jump or guide shattering.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, valve inspection, guide replacement, and decarbonising after minor chain jump has occurred.
repairOptions.longevity: High labour cost with limited long-term benefit if bottom end is compromised.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM651 Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine exchange with pre-fitted updated tensioner, full bench testing, and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed commercial-grade reliability; addresses root cause and protects remaining drivetrain.

problems.recommendation: On a Mercedes Benz Sprinter generating revenue, we strongly advise opting for the preventative chain kit if symptoms appear under 70,000 miles. Once the chain has jumped or metal debris contaminates the sump, a full rebuilt Mercedes Benz Sprinter OM651 replacement becomes the only financially viable route to avoid repeat failures.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz Sprinter

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Injector Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Injector Seal Failure - Why Carbon Buildup Destroys OM651 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 213 CDI, 314 CDI, 413 CDI, 514 CDI (2010-2018, OM651.955/OM651.958)
problems.rootCause: Original copper injector seat seals harden and crack, allowing high-pressure combustion gases to blow by. This rapidly carbonises the injector bore and cylinder head, eventually warping the sealing surface and causing coolant contamination.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Injector removal, bore machining, copper seal replacement, and cylinder head decarbonising.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective early intervention; fails to resolve issues if head sealing surface is already warped.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head off-site machining, new valve seats, pressure testing, and full gasket set replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores sealing but leaves high-mileage bottom end components at risk of future fatigue.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM651 Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete exchange unit featuring upgraded injector sleeves, new seals, and validated compression across all cylinders.
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates blow-by risk entirely; ideal for high-mileage vans requiring zero-downtime reliability.

problems.recommendation: If blow-by is detected early via coolant pressure loss or minor misfires, a specialist injector seal refurbishment on your Mercedes Benz Sprinter can extend service life affordably. Once carbonisation reaches the cylinder head mating surface, attempting further head machining is false economy; a rebuilt OM651 exchange unit offers superior longevity and immediate warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM651 injector failure repair or replacement on your Mercedes Benz Sprinter

problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Turbocharger Failure - Why OM642 V6 Engines Lose Oil and Power
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 319 CDI, 419 CDI, 519 CDI (2009-2018, OM642.896/OM642.898)
problems.rootCause: The turbocharger centre bearing oil seals degrade due to extended idling and heavy towing cycles, causing oil to migrate into the intake and exhaust. This starves the turbo of lubrication while contaminating intercoolers and DPF systems, ultimately leading to seized turbine shafts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo removal, bearing replacement with high-temp seals, shaft balancing, and intake system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before shaft play develops; requires strict oil change intervals post-repair.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Turbo Replacement & DPF Clean
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New OEM-spec turbo installation, intercooler replacement, and forced DPF regeneration cycle.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure but does not address potential bottom-end oil starvation damage.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM642 V6 Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete V6 exchange unit with reinforced turbo feed lines, updated oil coolers, and full bench load testing.
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for fleet vans; eliminates oil migration risk and secures 12‑month commercial warranty.

problems.recommendation: For Mercedes Benz Sprinter 319/519 CDI operators, replacing only the turbo is rarely a permanent fix once oil has contaminated the DPF and intercooler. We recommend a full rebuilt OM642 V6 engine exchange when shaft play exceeds 0.5mm or exhaust smoke is consistent, as the cost of repeated ancillary damage quickly outweighs a single warranty-backed replacement.
problems.cta: Compare OM642 V6 rebuilt engine prices for your Mercedes Benz Sprinter

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Sprinter EGR Valve Clogging
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Sprinter EGR Valve Clogging - Why VS30 OM654 Engines Trigger Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 214 CDI, 314 CDI, 316 CDI, 516 CDI (2018-Present, OM654.920)
problems.rootCause: The low-pressure EGR cooler housing on newer OM654 units features complex internal baffles that trap soot rapidly under urban stop-start conditions. Coolant and exhaust gases mix when internal cracks develop, causing immediate DPF blockage and engine control unit fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical EGR decarbonisation, cooler pressure test, and updated ECU mapping to reduce soot production.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary relief; clogging typically recurs within 10,000 miles without hardware modification.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Cooler Replacement & DPF Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cooler unit swap with reinforced housing, new gaskets, and professional DPF ash cleaning.
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate fault but leaves engine vulnerable to recurrence under heavy urban duty cycles.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt OM654 Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete 2.0-litre exchange unit fitted with revised EGR hardware, updated coolant routing, and full emissions compliance testing.
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive fix for VS30 generation vans; guarantees compliance and delivers 12‑month unlimited mileage cover.

problems.recommendation: On newer Mercedes Benz Sprinter VS30 models, EGR clogging is a systemic design vulnerability rather than isolated wear. While cleaning provides short-term operational relief, we advise commercial operators experiencing repeated limp-mode faults to secure a rebuilt OM654 engine featuring the reinforced EGR assembly, avoiding costly recurring diagnostics and DPF replacements.
problems.cta: Get free quotes for OM654 EGR failure and engine replacement on your Mercedes Benz Sprinter

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Timing Chain Stretch
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Timing Chain Stretch - Why M271.951 NGT Engines Suffer Catastrophic Failure
problems.rootCause: The dual-chain tensioner design on the CNG-capable M271.951 suffers from premature hydraulic pressure loss, allowing both intake and exhaust chains to elongate simultaneously. Without adequate tension, cam timing drifts, causing valve-to-piston contact and sudden engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front cover removal, complete timing kit replacement with upgraded hydraulic tensioners, and cam timing realignment.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective if performed before noise escalation; prevents internal valvetrain damage.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Head Inspection & Valve Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head off, bent valve replacement, seat machining, and compression verification post-chain jump.
repairOptions.longevity: Labour-intensive with uncertain long-term reliability; not recommended if pistons show impact marks.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M271.951 Engine Supply & Fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete natural gas/petrol exchange unit with reinforced timing components, validated compression, and 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Safest route for NGT fleet vehicles; eliminates stretch risk and restores dual-fuel efficiency immediately.

problems.recommendation: Given the precise engineering required for Mercedes Benz Sprinter NGT variants, we recommend the preventative dual-chain replacement at 60,000 miles as standard maintenance. Once metallic rattling or misfire codes appear, internal damage is usually irreversible, making a rebuilt M271.951 engine the most reliable and cost-effective solution for commercial operators.
problems.cta: Compare M271.951 rebuilt engine prices for your Mercedes Benz Sprinter 316 NGT

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz Sprinter
finalCta.paragraph: Engine failures on the Mercedes Benz Sprinter rarely happen without warning, but ignoring diagnostic codes or abnormal noises quickly turns a manageable repair into a fleet-disabling replacement. EngineMarket connects you directly with vetted UK Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine specialists who supply and fit rebuilt units nationwide, complete with upgraded components that neutralise known factory weaknesses. Every replacement engine carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, ensuring your commercial van returns to revenue-generating service without main dealer markups. Compare transparent pricing, verify specialist credentials, and secure your Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement today.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz Sprinter Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz Sprinter and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to the UK Mercedes Benz Sprinter lineup is detailed below, complete with compatible factory codes, production years, and documented failure notes to simplify your replacement search.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced in the 2018 VS30 generation to succeed the long-running 2.1-litre architecture, the OM654 block delivers tighter emissions compliance and refined cabin acoustics for modern commercial fleets. Its compact aluminium design and sequential turbocharging options provide the responsive torque required for heavy urban logistics and last-mile delivery cycles.
commonFailurePoints: coolant crossover leaks under sustained stop-start urban duty
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Sprinter 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: Serving as the commercial backbone of the W906 generation from 2006 to 2018, the OM651 powertrain replaced older five-cylinder designs with a highly efficient common-rail architecture that dominated UK van registrations. Its widespread adoption across payload classes made it the most frequently replaced commercial van engine due to high-mileage component fatigue and specific timing system vulnerabilities.
commonFailurePoints: Rear timing chain tensioner seal degradation (REC-005099 recall)
injector seat blow-by causing cylinder head carbonisation
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Sprinter 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Launched in 2009 to meet demand for heavy-towing, passenger shuttle, and premium coach conversion applications, the OM642 V6 introduced superior mid-range torque and acoustic refinement to the Sprinter range. Its robust iron block construction and dual-stage turbocharging make it a highly specified unit for operators requiring maximum payload capacity and 4x4 chassis stability.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger oil seal failure leading to intake contamination
oil cooler gasket deterioration around 100k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Sprinter 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Developed specifically for eco-conscious urban operators and strict emission zones, the M271 supercharged unit powers the factory 316 NGT variants engineered to run on compressed natural gas or standard petrol. Its compact inline-four layout and dedicated dual-fuel management system provide significantly reduced fuel taxation and running costs for city logistics fleets prioritising clean-air compliance.
commonFailurePoints: Dual timing chain stretch due to hydraulic tensioner wear
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Sprinter 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Sprinter 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced alongside the V6 diesel platform to serve international markets and LPG conversion specialists, the M272 powertrain delivers smooth, high-revving performance tailored for heavy passenger transport and minibus shuttle duties. Though less prevalent in the UK than its diesel equivalents, it remains a preferred specification for operators seeking quieter cabin operation and extended service intervals. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz Sprinter needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz Sprinter specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear causing excessive engine vibration
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Sprinter 3.5L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers every major engine change across the Mercedes Benz Sprinter's UK production history, organised so commercial operators can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices from specialist suppliers.

years.preview: W906 Launch: OM651 Four-Cylinder Diesel Debut
years.description: The W906 generation launched with the all-new OM651 2.1-litre diesel, replacing older five-cylinder units and establishing the engine family that would dominate UK Sprinter registrations for over a decade.
keyChanges: W906 platform replaces T1N generation with updated chassis and emissions compliance
OM651.950 2.1L diesel introduced as standard powertrain across 208-516 CDI variants
Common-rail injection and variable geometry turbocharging adopted for improved efficiency
Panel van, crew van, and chassis cab body styles launched simultaneously
mainEngines: OM651.950 - 2.1L diesel (208 CDI, 211 CDI, 311 CDI, 313 CDI early variants)
knownFor: OM651 timing chain tensioner seal vulnerability emerges in early production units
Robust bottom-end design delivers strong commercial mileage when maintained properly
Injector seat carbon buildup begins appearing on high-mileage urban delivery vehicles
Strong parts availability and specialist knowledge base develops rapidly across UK network
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2008 Mercedes Benz Sprinter replacement engines
years.ticker: 2006 Sprinter 313 CDI engine replacement • OM651 cold start rattle diagnosis • W906 timing chain tensioner leak fix
enquiries: W906 timing chain tensioner leak fix

years.preview: W906 V6 Introduction: OM642 Powers Heavy-Duty Sprinters
years.description: Mercedes-Benz expanded the Sprinter range with the OM642 3.0-litre V6 diesel, targeting high-payload, towing, and passenger shuttle applications requiring superior mid-range torque.
keyChanges: OM642.896 3.0L V6 diesel introduced for 319, 419, 519 CDI variants
BlueTEC emissions technology rolled out across diesel lineup
4x4/4MATIC drivetrain option added to selected chassis configurations
NGT (Natural Gas Technology) 316 variant launched with M271.951 petrol/CNG engine
mainEngines: OM642.896 - 3.0L V6 diesel (319, 419, 519 CDI heavy-duty variants)
knownFor: OM642 V6 delivers smooth power but develops turbo oil seal leaks around 100k miles
M271.951 NGT timing chain stretch becomes documented failure point for urban fleets
OM651 injector seal blow-by complaints increase as first high-mileage units reach replacement age
V6 variants command premium resale values for passenger conversion and towing applications
years.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Sprinter 2009-2011 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Sprinter 319 CDI V6 engine replacement • 316 NGT timing chain rattle fix • OM642 turbo oil leak repair cost
enquiries: OM642 turbo oil leak repair cost

years.description: Mid-cycle updates brought revised engine mapping, updated emissions hardware, and the OM651.958 code for higher-output 316/516 CDI variants requiring improved thermal management.
keyChanges: OM651.958 code introduced for facelift 316/516 CDI with updated BlueTEC calibration
Revised EGR cooler housing design deployed across OM651 family
Interior and exterior styling updates with enhanced driver assistance options
Extended service intervals introduced for commercial fleet operators
mainEngines: OM651.955 - 2.1L diesel (211-416 CDI standard output variants)
OM651.958 - 2.1L diesel (316, 416, 516 CDI high-output BlueTEC variants)
OM642.898 - 3.0L V6 diesel (319, 519 CDI updated calibration)
knownFor: OM651.958 shows improved thermal management but retains timing chain tensioner vulnerability
EGR cooler cracking reports increase as Euro 5 compliance hardware ages under commercial loads
High-output variants (163bhp) experience accelerated turbo wear on stop-start urban routes
Specialist rebuilders begin offering updated tensioner kits as preventative upgrade option
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2013 Mercedes Benz Sprinter OM651 replacement engines

years.preview: W906 Recall Era: OM651 Timing Chain Tensioner Campaign
years.description: Global recall REC-005099 targeting OM651 timing chain tensioner seal failures defined this period, driving unprecedented replacement enquiries for 313/316 CDI variants across the UK commercial van sector.
keyChanges: Mercedes-Benz issues recall REC-005099 for OM651 timing chain tensioner seal (5,500+ vehicles globally)
Updated tensioner part number A6510103500 deployed as warranty remedy
Injector seal part A6510170320 updated to address carbon buildup complaints
EGR cooler part A6510900215 reinforced following field failure analysis
mainEngines: OM651.958 - 2.1L diesel (316, 516 CDI high-output, recall-affected units)
OM642.898 - 3.0L V6 diesel (319, 519 CDI, turbo seal monitoring)
knownFor: Cold start rattle becomes primary diagnostic symptom for OM651 tensioner failure
Commercial operators prioritise 48-hour turnaround replacement to minimise revenue loss
Reconditioned OM651 units with updated tensioner kits become preferred solution over repair
12-month unlimited mileage warranty emerges as non-negotiable requirement for fleet buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2017 Mercedes Benz Sprinter OM651 recall replacement engines
years.ticker: OM651 timing chain recall check • Sprinter 313 CDI cold start rattle fix • 2014 Sprinter engine replacement cost UK
enquiries: Sprinter 313 CDI cold start rattle fix

years.preview: VS30/W907 Launch: OM654 Modern Diesel Architecture
years.description: The all-new VS30 generation introduced the downsized OM654 2.0-litre diesel with aluminium block, sequential turbocharging options, and revised emissions architecture for Euro 6d-TEMP compliance.
keyChanges: VS30 platform replaces W906 with updated chassis, electronics, and driver assistance systems
OM654.920 2.0L diesel introduced as replacement for OM651 across 214-516 CDI variants
48-volt mild-hybrid preparation integrated into electrical architecture
Revised cabin layout with MBUX infotainment and enhanced connectivity options

OM642.898 - 3.0L V6 diesel (carryover for 319/519 CDI heavy-duty applications)
knownFor: OM654 low-pressure EGR valve clogging emerges as primary failure mode under urban duty cycles

VS30 electronics complexity increases specialist diagnostic requirements for engine replacement

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz Sprinter Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mercedes Benz Sprinter OM651 engine typically costs £2,995-£4,200 supply-only, with supply-and-fit adding £450-£850 labour. V6 OM642 units range £4,000-£6,000 due to complexity. Prices vary by variant, engine code, and supplier location across the UK network. All engines include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted specialists.
keyPoints: OM651 2.1L diesel rebuilds average £2,995-£4,200 for 313/316 CDI variants
OM642 3.0L V6 replacements cost £4,000-£6,000 for 319/519 CDI models
Supply-and-fit labour typically adds £450-£850 depending on workshop location
Reconditioned units include updated tensioners addressing recall REC-005099
VS30 OM654 engines command premium pricing due to newer technology
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine prices from UK specialists now

items.question: What causes the OM651 timing chain failure in Mercedes Benz Sprinter vans?
items.answer: The OM651 timing chain tensioner seal degrades under thermal cycling, losing oil pressure and allowing chain slack. This causes guide rail breakage and potential valve damage, typically between 60k-110k miles. Mercedes issued recall REC-005099 covering 5,500+ vehicles globally for this specific failure pattern.
keyPoints: Rear-mounted tensioner seal cracks from repeated heating and cooling cycles
Oil pressure loss allows timing chain to stretch and jump teeth
Cold start rattling is the primary warning symptom before catastrophic failure
Updated tensioner part A6510103500 resolves the design vulnerability permanently
Failure risk peaks between 60,000 and 110,000 miles on high-mileage commercial use
items.cta: Get quotes for OM651 timing chain replacement or rebuilt engine for your Mercedes Benz Sprinter

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine or should I buy a new van?
items.answer: For most Mercedes Benz Sprinter owners, engine replacement offers significantly better value than purchasing a new or used van. A £3,000-£5,500 rebuilt OM651 extends vehicle life by 80k-120k miles, versus £25,000-£40,000 for a new Sprinter or £8,000-£15,000 for an untested used replacement.
keyPoints: Rebuilt OM651 engines typically deliver 80,000-120,000 additional miles of reliable service
Commercial operators save £15,000-£30,000 versus purchasing a new Sprinter van
Reconditioned units include warranty coverage protecting against repeat failure costs
Downtime for engine swap is typically 48 hours versus weeks sourcing a replacement vehicle
Retaining your known-service-history van avoids hidden faults in used alternatives
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement costs against van purchase prices today

items.question: How long does a Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement take?
items.answer: A professional Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine replacement typically takes 48-72 hours from collection to return, depending on parts availability and workshop capacity. Supply-only deliveries can arrive within 24 hours for common OM651 codes, while supply-and-fit bookings require advance scheduling with UK specialists.
keyPoints: Common OM651 engines often available for next-day delivery from UK stockists
Supply-and-fit installations typically complete within 48 hours at specialist workshops
VS30 OM654 units may require 3-5 days due to newer parts logistics
Mobile fitting services can reduce downtime by working at your business location
Pre-booking during off-peak periods secures faster turnaround for commercial operators
items.cta: Find Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine supply-and-fit specialists with 48-hour turnaround near you

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine is most reliable - OM651, OM642 or OM654?
items.answer: The OM654 2.0L diesel in VS30 generation Mercedes Benz Sprinter vans shows the best reliability record, benefiting from lessons learned on earlier OM651 and OM642 units. However, properly maintained OM651 engines with updated tensioner seals remain dependable workhorses for high-mileage commercial use across the UK.
keyPoints: OM654 features reinforced EGR housing and improved thermal management reducing failure risk
OM651 reliability depends critically on timely tensioner seal replacement per recall REC-005099
OM642 V6 delivers smooth power but requires vigilant turbo oil seal monitoring
All three engines achieve 150,000+ miles with proper servicing and quality oil changes
Rebuilt units with upgraded components outperform original factory specifications for longevity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651, OM642 and OM654 engine prices for your Mercedes Benz Sprinter variant

items.question: Does a reconditioned Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every reconditioned Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour. This commercial-grade coverage matches fleet operator requirements and protects against repeat failures, provided installation follows specialist guidelines and service intervals are maintained.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty is standard across EngineMarket's UK specialist network
Coverage includes parts and labour for defects in reconditioned engine components
Warranty remains valid when installed by qualified technicians following manufacturer procedures
Regular oil changes using correct specification fluids are required to maintain coverage
Claims processing typically completes within 48 hours to minimise commercial downtime
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Sprinter reconditioned engines with 12-month unlimited warranty

items.question: Can I fit a Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine myself or do I need a specialist?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced mechanics, fitting a Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine requires specialist tools, diagnostic equipment, and knowledge of VS30 electronics that most DIY operators lack. Professional installation ensures warranty validity, correct ECU coding, and avoids costly errors that can damage new or reconditioned units.
keyPoints: VS30 generation Sprinters require proprietary diagnostic tools for ECU adaptation and coding
Engine mounting points and ancillary routing differ significantly between W906 and VS30 chassis
Incorrect torque specifications or fluid filling can void warranty and cause premature failure
Specialist workshops carry insurance covering installation errors, protecting your investment
Supply-and-fit packages often cost less than DIY when factoring tool hire and time
items.cta: Find vetted Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine fitting specialists near your location today

items.question: What's the difference between used and reconditioned Mercedes Benz Sprinter engines?
items.answer: Used Mercedes Benz Sprinter engines are removed from donor vehicles with unknown service history and mileage, while reconditioned units undergo complete strip, inspection, and replacement of worn components like tensioners and seals. Reconditioned engines include warranty coverage and updated parts addressing known failure points like OM651 timing issues.
keyPoints: Reconditioned OM651 engines include updated tensioner seals addressing recall REC-005099
Used engines carry no warranty and may fail within weeks of installation
Reconditioned units are bench-tested under load before dispatch to verify performance
Price difference (£1,200-£2,500 used vs £2,995-£4,200 reconditioned) reflects reliability assurance
Commercial operators prioritise reconditioned units to avoid repeat downtime costs
items.cta: Compare used versus reconditioned Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine prices and warranty terms

items.question: How do I find my Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine code?
items.answer: Your Mercedes Benz Sprinter engine code appears on the engine identification plate near the timing cover, on the V5C registration document under section D.2, or by entering your registration on the DVLA vehicle enquiry service. Common codes include OM651.955 for 313 CDI and OM642.896 for 319 CDI variants.
keyPoints: Engine plate location: passenger side timing cover or near oil filter housing
V5C registration document section D.2 lists the official engine code for your vehicle
DVLA online vehicle enquiry confirms engine code using your number plate
OM651.955 powers most 313/316 CDI W906 Sprinters from 2006-2018
VS30 generation vans use OM654.920 code for 2.0L diesel variants
items.cta: Enter your Mercedes Benz Sprinter registration to instantly identify engine code and compatible replacements

items.question: Are Mercedes Benz Sprinter OM651 engines compatible with Vito or other models?
items.answer: The OM651 engine family appears across multiple Mercedes-Benz commercial vehicles including Vito, V-Class, and some passenger cars, but Mercedes Benz Sprinter installations have model-specific mounting points, ECU calibration, and ancillary routing. Direct swaps require specialist verification to ensure compatibility with your Sprinter's chassis generation and electronics.
keyPoints: OM651 block architecture is shared but Sprinter-specific ancillaries differ from Vito applications
ECU software calibration must match Sprinter chassis generation (W906 vs VS30) for proper operation
Mounting points, exhaust routing, and cooling system connections vary between model platforms
Using a non-Sprinter OM651 may require extensive modification voiding warranty coverage
EngineMarket specialists verify cross-model compatibility before dispatch to prevent installation issues
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Sprinter-specific OM651 engines with verified compatibility from UK specialists
